title: GitHub Guard Reddit bot score could be improved
author: jeffgca
body:
If you paste a link to this GitHub repo in some subreddits a bot called GitHub Guard may respond with a report: 

https://www.reddit.com/r/MacOS/s/YIIW3Ye2XK

The report is mostly positive but could be improved by adding a security policy, see more here:

https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/how-tos/report-and-fix-vulnerabilities/configure-vulnerability-reporting/add-security-policy

title: When using Thaw and Rectangle is hidden, then Rectangle sees Thaw as active app.
author: bric3
body: - [x] This is a new bug that hasn't been documented in a closed issue or in the Readme. Actually, it's not really a software but an interaction issue I think. macOS version: 26.5.1 Rectangle version: 0.96.0 Logs if applicable (In Rectangle menu, hold option, "View Logging..."): Thaw: https://github.com/stonerl/Thaw **Description:** Thaw is a menu bar management tool, in particular it allows show or hide items. When the items are hidden, Thaw, will either show * a popup, if there's not enough room like on macbook laptop with the notch * show menu bar items in the menu bar, e.g. on an external screen with enough room The following happens if Rectangle is _hidden_. Weirdly when there is not enough room, on the screen with a notch, and there is a popup, the Rectangle menu correctly find the active app. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5f765bb3-132a-43d3-8554-107e9d41038a However when there's enough room the menu items are show "inline" but then Rectangle is showing `Thaw` as the active app. And as such any actions apply to Thaw, not the application that was active before. THis might well be a Thaw issue, but I'd rather mention the issue here as well for visibility.

title: Snapping zones not working for Sidecar mode (macOS 27.0)
author: RicBent
body:
macOS 27.0 now allows regular touch input when using an iPad in Sidecar mode. That includes moving around windows.

When moving windows to screen edges using Sidecar, the snapping zones are currently not triggered.

macOS version: 27.0 Beta 2
Rectangle version: v0.89 (95)

title: Screen ordering is different from macOS preference
author: xmcp
body: - [x] This is a new bug that hasn't been documented in a closed issue or in the Readme. macOS version: 26.5.1 Rectangle version: 0.96 Logs if applicable (In Rectangle menu, hold option, "View Logging..."): Not applicable This is yet another screen ordering issue, similar to #1272, #1583 and #1595, but this time with a more unusual display configuration: Here the MacBook (labeled 2) is connected to two external displays (labeled 1 and 3). The system preference chooses the order 1-2-3, which is intuitive because 3 has more pixels on the right side of 2 than left side. However, the current algorithm of Rectangle sorts the display as 1-3-2 when OrderedByX is enabled, because it only considers the left corner of a display (`screen.frame.origin.x`). We can: - Change `screen.frame.origin.x` to `screen.frame.midX` in OrderedByX mode to better mimic the system behavior. - Or leave a terminal flag for advanced users to manually specify the order.

title: Add "cycle sides" repeat behavior for left/right half (parity with first-two-thirds)
author: statico
body:
- [x] This is a new bug that hasn't been documented in a closed issue or in the Readme.

(Thanks for making Rectangle! It's great.)

Repeatedly pressing the `firstTwoThirds` shortcut toggles the window between the left and right two-thirds of the screen. `firstThird` and `firstFourth` behave the same way. However, the `leftHalf` / `rightHalf` shortcuts have no equivalent "flip to the opposite side on repeat" behavior, which feels inconsistent.

Currently, with `leftHalf` bound to a single shortcut, repeating it cycles through displays or sizes, but there is no option to toggle the window to the right half on a single display.

`FirstTwoThirdsCalculation` (and the matching third/fourth calculations) inspects the last sub-action and automatically toggles between `leftTwoThirds` and `lastTwoThirds`. I would naturally expect the same toggling from `leftHalf` ↔ `rightHalf`, especially since the half commands are the most-used.

Current workarounds:
- Bind both halves to separate nearby shortcuts
- Use `firstTwoThirds` instead, accepting the wider window

Related issues:
- #973 — proposed direction-based cycling; was resolved by adding the same-side resize cycle (1/2 → 2/3 → 1/3), which doesn't flip to the opposite half.
- #110 — "Quick way to swap two halves of a screen" — closed with no description; title is ambiguous and no `swap` action exists in the source today.
- #1297 / #1287 / #1738 — bug reports about existing cycling, not feature requests for side-flipping.

macOS version: 26.4.1

Rectangle version: v.0.95 (101)

Logs if applicable (In Rectangle menu, hold option, "View Logging..."): 


title: Rectangle unresponsive when LanguageTool app is running
author: JCDC85
body:
macOS version: 26.4.1 
Rectangle version: 3.78 (223)
Logs if applicable (In Rectangle menu, hold option, "View Logging..."): 

This is my 2nd round trying to use Rectangle. I tried last month and had the same issue so I never moved forward with Pro. I saw there was a recent update so I wanted to try again.

The Rectangle settings window becomes extremely laggy, to the point of unresponsive, and the Snap Areas Feature doesnt work at all, while keyboard shortcuts and Throw still work.
Window Snapping via drag to edge works fine from other apps like BetterTouchTool and macOS window Tiling feature, but becomes unresponsive via Rectangle whenever the app LanguageTool is running. (https://languagetool.org). Specifically "LanguageTool for Desktop.app", not the LanguageTool.app.
Quitting LT is the only thing that fixes Rectangle, and starting up LT breaks it again immediately. I have quit every other app I have 1 at a time but the only one that impacts Rectangle is LT. 
I will be sending this behavior to the LT developer as well in hopes that one or both of you have the ability to fix it.
I am perplexed on why an app like LT, a text autocomplete app, would interact with Rectangle in this way at all.

title: Optional "Recently Used" Actions Section in Menu Bar Dropdown
author: amdn
body:
**Description:**

It would be great to have an optional setting that displays recently used actions at the top of the menu bar dropdown menu.

**Use Case:**

For users with large screens who frequently use actions like **Sixths**, navigating to those options each time requires scrolling down the list and then making a secondary submenu selection. While this is perfectly workable, having recently used actions pinned to the top would streamline repetitive workflows considerably.

**Proposed Behavior:**
- Optional toggle in Preferences (off by default, so it doesn't affect existing users)
- Tracks the last N recently used actions (N could also be configurable, e.g. 3–5)
- Displays them as a distinct section at the top of the menu, perhaps separated by a divider and labeled "Recent"
- Persists across app restarts via UserDefaults

**Alternatives Considered:**
- Keyboard shortcuts work well but require memorizing additional shortcuts for less frequently used actions like Sixths layouts

**Who Would Benefit:**
- Users with large displays who regularly use more specialized layouts (Sixths, Thirds, etc.) that are deeper in the menu hierarchy

title: alwaysAccountForStage not working correctly
author: mytrhan
body:
- [x] This is a new bug that hasn't been documented in a closed issue or in the Readme.

Hey, after setting up: defaults write `com.knollsoft.Rectangle alwaysAccountForStage -bool true` stage manager space isn't respected by maximize, left half, etc.

Mainly happening on fresh reboot or switching to an empty Workspace, where I have no other windows opened. To fix it I have to follow this sequence:
- Open first window in workspace, maximize, no stage manager space left
- Open second window, maximize, now stage manager space is left
- Go to first window, maximize again, now stage manager space is left

Is alwaysAccountForStage intended to always leave stage manager space even if there are no other windows? If that's the case it's not working correctly.

macOS version: Tahoe 26.4.1 
Rectangle version: 0.95
Logs if applicable (In Rectangle menu, hold option, "View Logging..."):

title: Window position resets to center when moving to next/previous display
author: munueuru
body:
Hi,

I'm using the "Move window to next display" and "Move window to previous display" shortcuts.

When I move a window that is positioned at a specific location (for example, right 1/3 of display 1) to another display, the window is placed in the center of the target display instead of keeping its relative position.

To clarify: the window size is preserved. Only the position changes to the center when moving between displays.

Example:
1. Window is at right 1/3 on Display 1
2. Move to Display 2 using "Next Display"
3. Window appears centered 1/3 on Display 2
4. Move back to Display 1
5. Window is still centered instead of returning to right 1/3

Expected behavior:
The window should keep the same relative position (e.g., right 1/3 → right 1/3) when moving between displays.

Is there a setting or planned feature to preserve window position across displays?

Thanks!
